steps.title: Use point-slope form
steps.content:
The line has slope 
$$
-\frac{4}{3}
$$ and passes through $(-9,0)$.
$$
y-0=-\frac{4}{3}(x-(-9))
$$
$$
y=-\frac{4}{3}(x+9)
$$

steps.title: Find the vertical intercept
steps.content: At the $y$-intercept, $x=0$. $$ y=-\frac{4}{3}(0+9) $$ $$ y=-\frac{36}{3}=-12 $$ The answer is -12.
